steps.title: Identify the angle relationship
steps.content: Lines l and k are parallel, and transversal t crosses both. Angles x and y are co-interior angles (same-side interior / consecutive interior angles) — they sit between the parallel lines on the same side of the transversal. The Co-Interior Angles Theorem states these always sum to 180°: $$x + y = 180°$$

steps.title: Apply the given constraint x > 100
steps.content: From $$x + y = 180°$$ we get $$y = 180° - x.$$ Since x > 100°: $$y = 180° - x \lt 180° - 100° = 80°$$ So y < 80° must always be true.

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'y < 80': As derived above, y = 180 − x and x > 100, so y < 80. This always holds. ✓
  • Choice B — 'y > 80': This would require y > 80, but we just showed y < 80 when x > 100. ✗
  • Choice C — 'x + y < 180': Co-interior angles always sum to exactly 180°, not less than 180°. ✗
  • Choice D — 'x + y > 180': Co-interior angles always sum to exactly 180°, not greater than 180°. ✗

steps.title: Conclusion
steps.content: Since co-interior angles are supplementary (x + y = 180°) and x > 100°, we get y < 80°. That matches choice A — A is correct.
